km.London, Middlesex, England: loនដុន, មីឌឡិសេច, ប្រទេសអង់គ្លេស

zh.lifeSummary: Margaret' the Saxon, of London.於1109年出生在英國米德萊可倫敦。她與Aylwin fitz Leofstanssen of Londonstane結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1166年在她的家鄉享年57歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Margaret' the Saxon, of London.于1109年出生在英国米德萊可倫敦。她与Aylwin fitz Leofstanssen of Londonstane结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。她去世于1166年在她的家乡享年57岁。

nameMeanings.name: Margaret'
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

nameMeanings.name: the
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Dorothea  , also used independently.

nameMeanings.name: Saxon,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of Estonian Suurhans ‘big Hans’ (see Hans ).

variants.name: Saxton
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, in origin a local name from a place in Cambridgeshire. The place name derives from the genitive case of the Old Norse byname Kakkr (apparently a derivative of kokkr ‘lump’) + Old English tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’. The surname is well known, as it was borne by William Caxton ( ?1422–91 ), who established the first printing press in England, but the reason for its recent adoption as a given name is not clear.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

nameMeanings.name: London.
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: transferred use of the surname, derived from Lyndon, a place in the former county of Rutland (now part of Leicestershire), so called from Old English lind ‘linden, lime tree’ + dūn ‘hill’. Its modern use as a male given name owes something to the American president Lyndon Baines Johnson ( 1908–73 ).

en.lifeSummary: Margaret' the Saxon, of London. was born in 1109, in London, Middlesex, England. She married Aylwin fitz Leofstanssen of Londonstane. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died in 1166, in her hometown, at the age of 57.

fr.lifeSummary: Margaret' the Saxon, of London. est née en 1109, à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre. Elle a épousé Aylwin fitz Leofstanssen of Londonstane. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle est décédée en 1166, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 57 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Margaret' the Saxon, of London. wurde 1109 in London, Middlesex, England geboren. Sie hat Aylwin fitz Leofstanssen of Londonstane geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb 1166 im Alter von 57 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Margaret' the Saxon, of London. è nata il nel 1109 nel London, Middlesex, England. Lei sposato Aylwin fitz Leofstanssen of Londonstane. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì nel 1166, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 57 anni.

ja.London, Middlesex, England: イギリスミッドレセックスロンドン
ja.lifeSummary: Margaret' the Saxon, of London.は1109年にイギリスミッドレセックスロンドンで生まれた。Aylwin fitz Leofstanssen of Londonstaneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼女は1166年に故郷で57歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret' the Saxon, of London.(은)는 1109년 영국 미들레세스 런던에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Aylwin fitz Leofstanssen of Londonstane(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1166년 그녀의 고향에서 57세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Margaret' the Saxon, of London. nasceu em 1109, em London, Middlesex, Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com Aylwin fitz Leofstanssen of Londonstane. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ela faleceu em 1166, em sua cidade natal, com 57 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Margaret' the Saxon, of London. родилась в 1109 г. в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Aylwin fitz Leofstanssen of Londonstane. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла в 1166 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 57 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Margaret' the Saxon, of London. nació en 1109, en London, Middlesex, Inglaterra. Se casó con Aylwin fitz Leofstanssen of Londonstane. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió en 1166, en su pueblo, a la edad de 57 años.
